title: 8 natural essential oils for anxiety sufferers with palpitations

remedies.remedy_desc: Valeriana officinalis essential oils, also known as valerian root oil, has been traditionally used to help with palpitations due to its calming and sedative properties. The oil contains constituents such as valepotriates, isovaleric acid, and valerenic acid, which work together to slow down the heart rate and promote relaxation. These properties help to reduce anxiety and stress, common triggers for palpitations. When using Valeriana officinalis essential oils for palpitation, it's recommended to inhale the oil through steam inhalation or apply it topically to the pulse points after diluting with a carrier oil. Start with a small amount, about 5-7 drops, and gradually increase as needed.  To use the oil safely, always dilute it with a carrier oil, such as coconut or jojoba oil, and perform a patch test before applying it to the skin. If you're experiencing palpitations, it's essential to consult with a healthcare professional for proper diagnosis and treatment. Inhaling the oil through steam inhalation can be done by adding a few drops to a bowl of hot water, then inhaling the vapor for a few minutes.  When using Valeriana officinalis essential oils for palpitation, you may experience a decrease in heart rate and blood pressure, leading to a reduction in palpitation episodes. You may also feel more relaxed and calm, which can help to alleviate anxiety and stress. However, if you're experiencing frequent or severe palpitations, it's crucial to seek medical attention to rule out any underlying medical conditions.
remedies.image_alt: Valeriana officinalis herbal essential oils for palpitation

remedies.remedy_desc: Passiflora incarnata essential oils have been traditionally used to help alleviate palpitations, which are irregular heartbeats that can be a symptom of anxiety or stress. The oil contains properties like flavonoids and alkaloids, which work together to calm the nervous system and reduce anxiety levels. The main constituents of Passiflora incarnata essential oil are isovitexin, vitexin, and homoplantaginin, which are responsible for its sedative and anxiolytic effects.  To use Passiflora incarnata essential oil for palpitations, you can try the following actions: - Add a few drops of the oil to a diffuser and inhale it for 5-10 minutes, ideally in the morning and evening. - Mix a few drops of the oil with a carrier oil like coconut or jojoba oil, and massage it onto your wrists, temples, or behind your ears. - Incorporate a few drops of the oil into your bath water for a relaxing soak. By taking these actions, you may experience a decrease in anxiety and stress levels, which can lead to a reduction in palpitations. The calming effects of the oil may also promote better sleep quality, improved mood, and increased feelings of relaxation. Additionally, you may notice a decrease in your heart rate and blood pressure, which can help alleviate palpitations. However, it's essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using Passiflora incarnata essential oil, especially if you have any underlying medical conditions or are taking medications.
remedies.image_alt: Passiflora incarnata herbal essential oils for palpitation

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia essential oils, also known as English lavender oil, has been traditionally used to help alleviate palpitations. The key reason for this is its high concentration of linalool and linalyl acetate, two compounds that have a calming effect on the nervous system. When inhaled, these constituents can help slow down heart rate and reduce anxiety, which often contribute to palpitations. The oil's sedative properties also help to regulate the body's stress response, allowing the heart to function normally. To use Lavandula angustifolia essential oils for palpitations, start by inhaling its fragrance through steam inhalation or using a diffuser. You can also add a few drops to a warm bath or apply it topically to your pulse points (diluted with a carrier oil, of course). If you're experiencing palpitations, try to remain calm and focus on your breath. When you inhale the oil's fragrance, take slow, deep breaths, and try to release any tension or stress you may be feeling. As you use Lavandula angustifolia essential oils, you may start to notice a decrease in heart rate and a reduction in palpitations. The oil's calming effects can help you relax and feel more centered, allowing your heart to function normally. You may also experience a decrease in anxiety and stress levels, making it easier to manage palpitations in the long run.
remedies.image_alt: Lavandula angustifolia herbal essential oils for palpitation

remedies.remedy_desc: Avena sativa essential oils, also known as oat seed oil, is a natural remedy that may help alleviate palpitations. The oil is rich in beta-sitosterol, a plant sterol that has been shown to have a calming effect on the nervous system. It also contains a compound called avenacoside, which has anti-anxiety properties. When inhaled, the oil's active compounds can help slow down heart rate and reduce stress levels, making it easier to manage palpitations. To use Avena sativa essential oils for palpitation, you can add a few drops to a diffuser or mix it with a carrier oil for topical application. Start with a small amount, as you can always increase the dosage later. It's recommended to inhale the oil for 5-10 minutes, 2-3 times a day. As you take action, you may notice a reduction in heart palpitations, improved sleep quality, and a decrease in anxiety levels. Additionally, you may experience a sense of calmness and relaxation, making it easier to manage stress. However, if your palpitations persist or worsen, it's essential to consult with a healthcare professional for proper diagnosis and treatment.
remedies.image_alt: Avena sativa herbal essential oils for palpitation

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale essential oils, also known as ginger essential oil, has been traditionally used to help alleviate palpitations. This is due to its active compounds, such as gingerol and shogaol, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These compounds help to calm the nervous system, reducing anxiety and stress that can contribute to palpitations. Ginger essential oil also has a positive effect on the heart, improving blood circulation and reducing inflammation in the cardiovascular system. If you're experiencing palpitations, you can try adding a few drops of ginger essential oil to a diffuser or applying it topically to your skin (diluted with a carrier oil). To do this, gently massage a few drops of the oil into your temples, wrists, or behind your ears. You can also inhale the oil directly from the bottle or add it to a warm bath for a relaxing effect. When using ginger essential oil, start with a small amount and gradually increase as needed. When you take action with ginger essential oil, you may experience a reduction in palpitation frequency and intensity. Your heart rate may also become more stable, and you may feel a sense of calm and relaxation. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory properties of ginger essential oil may help to reduce swelling and pain in the chest area, providing relief from palpitations. Regular use of ginger essential oil may also help to regulate your heartbeat and improve overall cardiovascular health.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal essential oils for palpitation

remedies.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis essential oils, also known as lemon balm oil, has been traditionally used to help alleviate palpitations. The main reason for this is its calming and sedative properties, which come from constituents like linalool, linalyl acetate, and beta-pinene. These compounds have a relaxing effect on the nervous system, which can help slow down the heart rate and reduce anxiety. To use Melissa officinalis essential oils for palpitations, you can try the following: inhale the oil directly from the bottle, add a few drops to your bath water, or mix it with a carrier oil and apply it to your skin. When inhaling, take slow and deep breaths through your nose and out through your mouth. For a bath, soak for at least 20 minutes to allow the oil to take effect. If you're applying it to your skin, do a patch test first and start with a small amount. When you start using Melissa officinalis essential oils for palpitations, you may feel a sense of calmness and relaxation. Your heart rate may slow down, and you may experience a reduction in anxiety. Some people may also report improved sleep quality and reduced symptoms of stress. It's essential to note that if you experience severe or persistent palpitations, you should consult a healthcare professional for proper diagnosis and treatment.
remedies.image_alt: Melissa officinalis herbal essential oils for palpitation

remedies.remedy_desc: Piper nigrum essential oils, also known as black pepper essential oil, has been traditionally used to help alleviate palpitations. This is due to its analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which help to calm the nervous system and reduce anxiety. The main constituents of Piper nigrum essential oil include limonene, beta-pinene, and beta-caryophyllene, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. When experiencing palpitations, inhaling a few drops of Piper nigrum essential oil in a diffuser or applying it topically to the pulse points (such as wrists or temples) can help to calm the heart rate and reduce anxiety. To use Piper nigrum essential oil for palpitations, start by inhaling 2-3 drops of the oil in a diffuser for 5-10 minutes, or apply a few drops to your wrists or temples. You may also add a few drops to a warm bath or inhale it directly from a cloth or handkerchief. If you experience palpitations, try to stay calm, sit or lie down in a quiet space, and focus on deep breathing exercises while inhaling the oil. By doing so, you may be able to slow down your heart rate and reduce anxiety, leading to a decrease in palpitations and an overall sense of calmness.
remedies.image_alt: Piper nigrum herbal essential oils for palpitation

remedies.remedy_desc: Centella asiatica essential oils, also known as Gotu Kola, may help with palpitations due to its calming and balancing properties. The oil contains constituents like asiatic acid, madecassic acid, and brahmic acid, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. These properties may help reduce anxiety and stress that can contribute to palpitations.  When using Centella asiatica essential oils for palpitations, start by inhaling the oil through steam inhalation or apply it topically to the temples, wrists, or chest. You can mix a few drops of the oil with a carrier oil like coconut or jojoba oil and apply it to your skin. If you're using it as a diffuser, add 5-7 drops to the diffuser and inhale the oil for 5-10 minutes. When you inhale the oil or apply it topically, you may experience a sense of calmness and relaxation, which can help slow down your heart rate and reduce palpitations. The anti-inflammatory properties of the oil may also help reduce anxiety and stress, making it easier to breathe and relax. Some people may also notice an improvement in sleep quality, which can further contribute to reducing palpitations. However, it's essential to note that if you experience severe palpitations or any other symptoms, you should consult a healthcare professional for proper diagnosis and treatment.
remedies.image_alt: Centella asiatica herbal essential oils for palpitation

intro_desc: Anxiety can be a real nightmare for many people. It can make them feel like they're walking on eggshells, constantly worried about what might happen next. And for some, anxiety can also cause a racing heart, known as palpitations. These uncomfortable feelings can be a huge problem for anxiety sufferers, making it hard for them to go about their daily lives. They might feel like they're on edge all the time, and even small things can trigger a panic attack. This can make it hard to work, spend time with loved ones, or even take care of basic needs like eating and sleeping. It's like their body is constantly on high alert, and they can't seem to calm down.  When anxiety causes palpitations, it's often because the body's "fight or flight" response is triggered. This response is meant to help us react quickly to danger, but for anxiety sufferers, it can be triggered by things that aren't actually life-threatening. This can lead to a buildup of stress hormones like adrenaline, which can make the heart beat faster and feel like it's pounding in the chest. It's like the body is stuck in a perpetual state of emergency, and it doesn't know how to turn off the alarm.  But there is some good news. Herbal essential oils have been shown to be a helpful tool in calming the body and reducing anxiety symptoms like palpitations. These oils are derived from plants and can be inhaled or applied to the skin to promote relaxation and reduce stress. They can help slow down the heart rate and calm the nervous system, making it easier to breathe and feel more centered. Some of these oils have a natural sedative effect, while others can help reduce anxiety by promoting feelings of calm and relaxation. By using these oils in conjunction with other relaxation techniques, anxiety sufferers may be able to find some relief from their palpitations and other symptoms.
